title: Financial Recognition Scheme for Veterans Affected by LGBT Ban Announced

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content:
The UK government has launched the LGBT Financial Recognition Scheme aimed at supporting veterans who were affected by the Ban on LGBT personnel in HM Armed Forces, which was in place from 1967 to 2000. This initiative seeks to provide financial recognition to those who served during this period and faced discrimination due to their sexual orientation.

The scheme is part of the government’s ongoing commitment to address historical injustices faced by members of the armed forces. Veterans are encouraged to access guidance on the application process and eligibility criteria, ensuring they receive the acknowledgment they deserve. Scotland’s rich aviation heritage, including the participation of LGBT individuals in various roles within the military, highlights the importance of inclusivity in all sectors.

sourceReferences.citationText: mod-press-office – Guidance: Veterans of the LGBT Ban: Financial Recognition Scheme
